THE TORNADO BEGAN 1.4 MILES WNW OF DORENA IN OPEN FARMLAND JUST NORTH OF HIGHWAY 102 AND UPROOTED AND SNAPPED SOME LARGE TREES ALONG HIGHWAY 77, AND PROCEEDED EAST-NORTHEASTWARD TOWARD THE MISSISSIPPI RIVER, DAMAGING A PIVOT SPRINKLER SYSTEM AND TOTALLY DESTROYING AN EQUIPMENT SHED NEAR THE LEVEE. MANY TREES ON THE EAST SIDE OF THE LEVEE HAD TRUNKS AND LIMBS SNAPPED, AND SOME TREES WERE UPROOTED. MAXIMUM PATH WIDTH 400 YARDS. PATH LENGTH 3.8 MILES.
LATE REPORT. TWO FARM BUILDINGS DESTROYED, TREES DOWN, AND A PIVOT FLIPPED. TIME ESTIMATED FROM RADAR.

AN EF-1 TORNADO WITH ESTIMATED PEAK WINDS OF 105 MPH STRUCK HICKMAN COUNTY, KY, EARLY TUESDAY MORNING. MULTIPLE TREES WERE SNAPPED. MANY TREE BRANCHES AND SOME ENTIRE TREE FELL ONTO SEVERAL HOMES, WHICH INFLICTED DAMAGE. THE TORNADO BEGAN AT 3:29 AM 7 MILES WEST OF CLINTON, KY, AND ENDED AT 3:38 AM JUST NORTHEAST OF CLINTON, KY.

EF-2 TORNADO WITH PEAK WINDS OF 125 MPH STRUCK FULTON COUNTY EARLY TUESDAY MORNING. ONE HOME WAS DESTROYED WITH ITS ROOF REMOVED AND SOME EXTERIOR WALLS KNOCKED DOWN. MULTIPLE OUTBUILDINGS COLLAPSED. MULTIPLE LARGE TREES SNAPPED AND UPROOTED. THE TORNADO BEGAN 5.2 MILES WEST NORTHWEST OF FULTON AND ENDED NEAR THE INTERSECTION OF US HIGHWAY 51 AND HIGHWAY 94.
MAJOR STRUCTURAL DAMAGE TO HOUSE. ROOF AND WALL OFF HOUSE. TREE ON HOUSE. CAR MOVED 40 FEET. POSSIBLE TORNADO.

ESTIMATED PEAK WINDS AT 95 MPH AND MAXIMUM PATH WIDTH WAS 125 YDS. SEVERAL FARM BUILDINGS LOST PARTS OR ALL OF THEIR ROOFS. SEVERAL SMALLER STRUCTURES DESTROYED.
